label: Day 4 — Morning: Creative Recycling Workshop
description: A hands-on creative session where participants transform waste materials into useful or beautiful objects. This session is deliberately less structured than others — it's about making, not analysing. The outputs become part of the venue's decoration and later the Green Map documentation.
objectives: Experience the creative potential of waste materials directly
Practice collaboration across language barriers through making
Document the process as content for the Green Map
Reconnect with physical, tactile creativity after screen-heavy sessions

steps.title: Inspiration gallery
steps.body: 5-minute slide show of 20 extraordinary upcycling projects from around the world. No explanation — just images. One question: which one surprised you most?

steps.title: Material marketplace
steps.body: All collected materials are laid out on a central table. Teams (4 people, mixed nationality) browse and choose their materials. No instructions yet — just looking and touching.

steps.title: Making time
steps.body: Teams create their object with no further instruction. Facilitator circulates, asks questions, documents with photos. Music playing. No evaluation, no grading — only making.

steps.title: Gallery walk & documentation
steps.body: Each team presents their object: what it is, what it was, what it could be used for. Other teams use the gallery documentation tool to record their observations for the Green Map.

facilitatorNote: Resist the urge to make this session more 'educational' with theory input. The making is the learning. Some groups will struggle with the lack of structure — that's fine. The productive discomfort of 'what do I do with this?' is exactly where creativity begins.
